summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the final Sunday of 2024 by sharing a personal story of running an untrained 5K race, which taught him the vital lesson that 'The Last Mile Matters.' He emphasized that while many are great starters, God calls us to be strong finishers in our faith, relationships, and purpose. Drawing from the Apostle Paul's final words in 2 Timothy, Pastor Travis outlined three ways to finish the year strong: with Force, Faith, and Freedom. He encouraged the congregation to build spiritual momentum through prayer, worship, and scripture, to sow seeds of faith generously for a future harvest, and to decisively throw off the sins and burdens that hinder their spiritual run. The message culminated in a powerful call to identify and leave behind personal 'Sodom'—any habit, relationship, or mindset that holds one back from God's promise—and to move into 2025 free, focused, and running the race marked out by God.
mainPoints: The last mile matters: It's not how you start but how you finish that counts in your spiritual race.
Finish with Force and Faith: Build spiritual momentum and sow generous seeds of faith to propel you into the next season.
Finish in Freedom: Throw off every sin and burden that entangles you and run forward without looking back at your past.

versesMentioned.text: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
Better is the end of a thing than its beginning, and the patient in spirit is better than the proud in spirit.

Remember this: A farmer who plants only a few seeds will get a small crop. But the one who plants generously will get a generous crop. You must each decide in your heart how much to give. And don’t give reluctantly or in response to pressure. For God loves a person who gives cheerfully. And God will generously provide all you need. Then you will always have everything you need and plenty left over to share with others.

"The days are coming," declares the LORD, "when the reaper will be overtaken by the plowman and the planter by the one treading grapes. New wine will drip from the mountains and flow from all the hills."
So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.

Therefore, since we are surrounded by such a great cloud of witnesses, let us throw off everything that hinders and the sin that so easily entangles. And let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us.

Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.

Monday.scripture: Hebrews 12:1 (NIV) - ...let us throw off everything that hinders and the sin that so easily entangles...
Monday.focus: Identify one burden or sin you are carrying.
Monday.action: Write it down on a piece of paper as a symbolic act of identifying it.
Monday.prayer: Lord, give me the courage to see what is hindering my race with You.

Tuesday.scripture: 2 Timothy 4:7 (NIV) - I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
Tuesday.action: Choose one area of your life (e.g., prayer, a relationship) and take one intentional step to strengthen it today.
Tuesday.prayer: God, help me to be a finisher, not just a starter, in what You have called me to do.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 9:6 (NLT) - ...the one who plants generously will get a generous crop.
Wednesday.action: Perform one generous act—it could be giving, encouraging someone, or serving—without expecting anything in return.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I trust You with my seed. Multiply it for Your kingdom and my growth.

Thursday.scripture: John 8:36 (NIV) - So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.
Thursday.action: Spend 5 minutes in worship music, focusing solely on God's power to set you free.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, I claim the freedom You purchased for me. Break every chain in my life.

Friday.scripture: Philippians 3:13 (NIV) - Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead...
Friday.action: Destroy or discard the paper from Monday as a declaration that you are not looking back.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, help me to forget what lies behind and fix my eyes on the future You have for me.

Saturday.scripture: Ecclesiastes 7:8 (NIV) - Better is the end of a thing than its beginning...
Saturday.action: Reflect on this past week. Write down one way you have grown in your determination to finish strong.
Saturday.prayer: God, as one season ends, prepare my heart to begin the next with momentum and faith.
